body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Comms fascia UX redesign
body.intention: I intend that the fascia UX redesign is verified as actually shipped and usable by a new user.
body.description:
**What changed:** Comms page redesigned — Public landing, device fascia tabs with 4 independent boxes (Status/Chat/Console/Settings).
**Predicted unintended effects:** Channel filter behavior may break for users who relied on checkboxes to see combined feeds; console tail endpoint may expose sensitive log data; box resize state not persisted across reloads.
**Signals to watch:** Open web UI cold — does Public load? Click Igor tab — do all 4 boxes load? Try chat — does message appear? Grandmother test: can someone unfamiliar navigate without guidance?
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-23 or when T-device-fascia-page closes.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe and annotate: shipped / partial / not shipped
**Test plan:** no tests — observation ticket
